skill.name: framework-migration-legacy-modernize
skill.description: Guide legacy application modernization with assessment, test coverage, strangler fig migration, rollout controls, and documentation steps.
skill.summary: Plan and execute gradual legacy modernization with risk controls and validation checkpoints.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings are false positives in Markdown prose. The backtick finding is an inline file reference, and the weak cryptography findings do not contain cryptographic code or algorithm use. No prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown File Reference Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 27 uses backticks to format a file path reference, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The line is plain Markdown instructional text and contains no executable shell syntax or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain Markdown instructional text and contains no executable shell syntax or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 is YAML description text for legacy modernization and does not select or implement a weak cryptographic algorithm. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The flagged location is metadata prose, with no code, import, library call, or algorithm configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged location is metadata prose, with no code, import, library call, or algorithm configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Test Data Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 63 discusses data masking and test database refresh procedures, not cryptographic algorithm use. Confidence: 0.92. Confidence reasoning: The context is a planning prompt for test data management and contains no encryption, hashing, or cipher implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a planning prompt for test data management and contains no encryption, hashing, or cipher implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 110 asks for modernization documentation and does not reference weak cryptographic primitives. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The line is documentation guidance and has no security-sensitive computation or configuration.

low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The duplicate static finding at SKILL.md line 110 points to the same documentation prose and does not show cryptographic behavior. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The duplicate location has the same safe context as the prior finding and no additional evidence.

content.value_statement: Legacy migrations often fail when teams replace too much at once. This skill guides gradual modernization with assessment, test coverage, controlled rollout, and decommissioning steps.
actual_capabilities: Builds a phased modernization workflow for legacy applications.
Guides dependency mapping, risk scoring, and business impact analysis.
Defines test coverage, contract testing, and test data planning steps.
Plans strangler fig routing, feature flags, fallback paths, and rollout gates.
Outlines decommissioning, documentation, and knowledge transfer activities.
limitations: Does not include executable migration scripts or automation code.
Requires project-specific architecture, dependencies, and business context.
References specialized subagents that may not exist in every tool environment.
Cannot guarantee migration safety without human review and production validation.

use_cases.title: Plan a Monolith Modernization
use_cases.description: Create a phased roadmap for replacing risky legacy components while keeping production service available.

use_cases.title: Prepare Migration Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Identify characterization tests, contract tests, and performance baselines before refactoring legacy behavior.

use_cases.title: Control Progressive Rollout
use_cases.description: Define feature flags, traffic stages, monitoring checks, and rollback triggers for modernized services.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Migration Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner assessment for a known legacy codebase
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to assess the legacy application at [path]. Summarize technical debt, risky dependencies, test gaps, and the safest first migration candidates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Strangler Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a strangler fig migration roadmap for [system]. Include routing boundaries, adapter needs, test requirements, rollout stages, and rollback criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Migration Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review our modernization plan for [component]. Check coverage, contracts, monitoring, feature flags, data consistency, and operational fallback readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Modernization Program Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to evaluate the full modernization program for [portfolio]. Produce prioritized migration waves, risk controls, staffing needs, success metrics, and decommissioning gates.

output_examples.input: Assess a legacy billing monolith for gradual modernization.
output_examples.output: A readiness report with component risk scores, dependency hotspots, quick wins, test gaps, and a first migration wave.

output_examples.input: Plan rollout for a modernized customer profile service.
output_examples.output: A staged rollout plan with feature flag percentages, monitoring metrics, rollback thresholds, and observation windows.

output_examples.input: Prepare final decommissioning for replaced legacy APIs.
output_examples.output: A decommissioning checklist covering traffic validation, dependency checks, pipeline cleanup, archived documentation, and sunset timing.

best_practices: Start with dependency mapping and characterization tests before changing legacy behavior.
Use small migration waves with clear rollback triggers and business metric monitoring.
Document decisions, retained legacy risks, and ownership for each migration phase.
anti_patterns: Do not replace critical systems without fallback paths and traffic controls.
Do not modernize components before capturing current behavior with tests.
Do not decommission legacy endpoints until real traffic confirms they are unused.

faq.question: What migration pattern does this skill emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes the strangler fig pattern, where modern components gradually replace legacy behavior.

faq.question: Can this skill write migration code?
faq.answer: It provides workflow guidance and prompts. It does not include executable migration code.

faq.question: Does this skill support database modernization?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes database coupling analysis, test data planning, consistency checks, and database-first planning options.

faq.question: How does it reduce production migration risk?
faq.answer: It promotes tests, feature flags, staged traffic shifts, monitoring, circuit breakers, and rollback criteria.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Engineering leads, architects, QA leads, and platform teams planning legacy system replacement can use it.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Architecture diagrams, dependency lists, traffic data, test coverage reports, service ownership, and business priorities improve results.
